title: Errors 1
description: Rust uses the `Result` type for recoverable errors. Functions can return `Ok(value)` or `Err(error)`.
instructions: Make the function return an error for invalid input instead of panicking.
hints: Use `Err(String)` to return an error value.
Use `Ok(String)` to return a success value.
Return Ok(format!("Hi! My name is {name}")) for the success case.
explanation: The `Result<T, E>` type is Rust's way of handling recoverable errors. Instead of panicking (crashing), functions return `Ok(value)` for success or `Err(error)` for failure. The caller must then handle both cases, typically with `match`. This makes error handling explicit and compile-time checked.
